title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland (Part 9)
titleZh: 爱丽丝梦游仙境（第9部分）

q.en: What is the Knave of Hearts accused of in the trial?
q.zh: 在审判中，红心杰克被指控犯了什么罪？

options.en: Stealing the tarts the Queen made
Breaking the croquet arches according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 弄坏槌球球门（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The White Rabbit reads: 'The Queen of Hearts, she made some tarts... The Knave of Hearts, he stole those tarts.'
explain.zh: 白兔宣读：'红心王后做了些馅饼……红心杰克偷走了那些馅饼。'

q.en: When the King tells the Hatter to take off his hat, what does the Hatter reply?
q.zh: 国王让帽匠脱帽时，帽匠怎么回答？

options.en: 'The Queen gave it to me' according to some versions of the story
options.zh: '是王后给我的'（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: 'It isn't mine'—he keeps hats to sell and is a hatter
options.zh: '那不是我的'——他留着帽子是要卖的，他是做帽子的

explain.en: The Hatter says, 'It isn't mine... I keep them to sell. I've none of my own. I'm a hatter.'
explain.zh: 帽匠说：'那不是我的……我留着它们是要卖的。我自己一顶也没有。我是做帽子的。'

q.en: When Alice whispers 'Stupid things!' about the jurors, what do they do?
q.zh: 爱丽丝小声说陪审员是'蠢东西！'时，他们做了什么？

options.en: They leave the court according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们离开了法庭（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They write 'stupid things!' down on their slates
options.zh: 他们在石板上写下'蠢东西！'

explain.en: Alice sees the jurors all writing down 'stupid things!' on their slates.
explain.zh: 爱丽丝看到陪审员们都在石板上写下了'蠢东西！'。

q.en: According to the Duchess's cook, what are the tarts mostly made of?
q.zh: 根据公爵夫人的厨娘的说法，果馅饼主要用什么做的？

options.en: Treacle according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 糖蜜（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The cook answers the King: 'Pepper, mostly.' (A sleepy Dormouse mutters 'Treacle.')
explain.zh: 厨娘回答国王：'主要是胡椒。'（昏昏欲睡的睡鼠嘟囔了一句'糖蜜'。）

q.en: According to the glossary, what does 'suppressed' mean, as when the guinea-pigs who cheered were silenced?
q.zh: 根据词汇表，当欢呼的几内亚猪被制止时，'suppressed'是什么意思？

options.en: Greatly praised according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 大加赞扬（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines suppressed as 'forcibly stopped or subdued.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 suppressed 定义为'被强行制止或制服'。

q.en: Carroll shapes Part 9 like a real trial—accusation, witnesses, jury, verdict—but fills it with nonsense. What does he do with this familiar form?
q.zh: 卡罗尔把第9部分写得像一场真实审判——指控、证人、陪审团、判决——却塞满了荒唐事。他如何处理这种熟悉的体裁？

options.en: He parodies the trial format for comedy, inverting its logic
options.zh: 他用戏仿审判体裁来制造喜剧，并颠倒其逻辑

options.en: He writes a serious legal document according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他写了一份严肃的法律文件（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: By mimicking a court then breaking its rules, Carroll parodies official trials.
explain.zh: 卡罗尔先模仿法庭、再打破其规则，以此戏仿正式的审判。

q.en: Alice has never been in a real court, yet she recognizes the judge, jury-box, and jurors from books. What does this show?
q.zh: 爱丽丝从没进过真正的法庭，却从书里认出了法官、陪审席和陪审员。这说明了什么？

options.en: She has a good memory only according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她只是记性好（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: She uses book knowledge to make sense of a strange situation
options.zh: 她用书本知识来理解陌生的情境

explain.en: Alice says she knows the names 'from books,' showing she connects reading to the world.
explain.zh: 爱丽丝说这些名称是'从书里'知道的，说明她把阅读所得联系到现实中。

q.en: The jury writes down the Hatter's three dates and 'added them up, and reduced the answer to shillings and pence.' What does this reveal?
q.zh: 陪审团把帽匠说的三个日期写下来，'加在一起，并换算成先令和便士'。这揭示了什么？

options.en: The trial treats unrelated things as if they were math—its logic is absurd
options.zh: 审判把不相干的东西当作数学来处理，其逻辑十分荒唐

options.en: The jury is good at math according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 陪审团擅长数学（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Adding dates like sums of money shows the court's reasoning is silly and mixed-up.
explain.zh: 把日期当钱来加总，说明法庭的推理既愚蠢又混乱。

q.en: Why does the author have the King declare 'Of course twinkling begins with a T!' and similar faulty logic?
q.zh: 作者为什么让国王宣称'闪烁(twinkling)当然是以 T 开头！'以及类似的错误推理？

options.en: To describe the weather according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了描述天气（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To mock authority figures and create comedy through bad reasoning
options.zh: 为了嘲弄权威人物，并用错误推理制造喜剧

explain.en: The King's silly certainties make him a figure of fun and amuse the reader.
explain.zh: 国王那种愚蠢的笃定让他成了笑料，也逗乐了读者。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in Part 9?
q.zh: 作者撰写第9部分的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To entertain by parodying a courtroom trial with absurd rules and characters
options.zh: 用荒唐的规则和角色戏仿一场法庭审判来娱乐读者

options.en: To explain how tarts are baked according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 解释果馅饼如何烘烤（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Part 9 delights readers with a mock trial full of silly rules and wordplay.
explain.zh: 第9部分用充满荒唐规则与文字游戏的模拟审判来取悦读者。

prompt.en: This courtroom scene is told from Alice's point of view. Rewrite the scene from the point of view of the King of Hearts, showing how he sees the trial.
prompt.zh: 这场法庭戏是从爱丽丝的视角讲述的。请以红心国王的视角重写这一场景，展现他眼中的审判。

en: Include one absurd rule you invent as King.
Show how you view Alice and the witnesses.
zh: 包含一条你以国王身份发明的荒唐规则。
写出你如何看待爱丽丝和各位证人。
